titlestmt.titleproper: Cornell University commencement programs and related materials37-8-346
titlestmt.author: Compiled by T.A. Gobert

publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:26:32 UTC.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

ead.archdesc:
======= RMA00346Cornell University commencement programs and related materials37-8-346Compiled by T.A. GobertDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20032B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
socscThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:43:07 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Cornell University commencement programs and related materials
Cornell University
37-8-346
5.5 cubic feet. 5.5 cubic feet.
1 videocassettes. 1 videocassette.
288.4 gigabytes. 288.4 GB
1869-2016. Included are announcements, invitations, tickets, programs, lists of degree candidates, and other items relating to Cornell University Commencement, Baccalaureate Services, Class Day celebrations amd Senior Week activities. Also some items pertaining to ceremonies held by various colleges of the university. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 18 - folder 1 18 - folder 2 7 (unprocessed) electronic records ======= 18 - folder 1 18 - folder 2 7 (unprocessed) electronic records >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2088117
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
T.A. Gobert
Date completed:
September 1988
EAD encoding:
Martin Heggestad, May 2003
Date modified:
Kristen Reichenbach, January 2019
COLLECTION ARRANGEMENT
Arranged chronologically by class year.
Cite As:
Cornell University commencement programs and class printed materials, #37-8-346. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Memoranda to Trustees and faculty on Baccalaureate and Commencement services from the Committee on Commencement Arrangements, 1960, 1963.
Scope and content
Carbon copy of President Malott's letter, November 4, 1957, inviting Wilbour Eddy Saunders, President of Colgate-Rochester Divinity School, to conduct the Cornell Baccalaureate Service on June 15, 1958; also two pamphlets giving program and information concerning the Service and Commencement Exercises.
RELATED MATERIALS
See also Cornell University student class printed memorabilia, #37-5-3019.
Menus. Programs (documents) Commencement ceremonies -- New York (State) -- Ithaca. College students. Cornell University -- : Students -- : New York (State) -- : Ithaca.

Commencement address by James A. Perkins and Commencement programClass of 1970June 8, 1970330
Scope and Contents
Instructions for the Academic Procession and the Commencement Ceremonies, Order of Proceedings for those taking part in the Conferring of Degrees, and Commencement Exercises and Investiture of the President. Includes pamphlet for the 1970 Commencement by the Committee of Concerned Seniors that discusses the Africana Studies Center fire, racism and financial inequality, Black Panthers, Vietnam War, Julius Stratton and MIT military work, excerpts from a poem, "Maybe the last time..." by Daniel Berrigan given during his Cornell farewell, info on the speaker Father David Connor, requests to contribute to Black Commitment Fund and to not wear a gap and gown to bring further awareness to these issues.Class of 1971June 7, 1971331
Scope and Contents
Instructions for the Procession and the Commencement Ceremonies and Commencement programClass of 1972May 26, 1972332

Commencement address by James A. Perkins and Commencement programClass of 1970June 8, 1970330
Scope and Contents
Instructions for the Academic Procession and the Commencement Ceremonies, Order of Proceedings for those taking part in the Conferring of Degrees, and Commencement Exercises and Investiture of the President. Includes pamphlet for the 1970 Commencement by the Committee of Concerned Seniors that discusses the Africana Studies Center fire, racism and financial inequality, Black Panthers, Vietnam War, Julius Stratton and MIT military work, excerpts from a poem, "Maybe the last time..." by Daniel Berrigan given during his Cornell farewell, info on the speaker Father David Connor, requests to contribute to Black Commitment Fund and to not wear a gap and gown to bring further awareness to these issues.Class of 1971June 7, 1971331
Scope and Contents
